evals.prompt: I'm setting up mini-SDD on this project for the first time. Please run mini-sdd-context to bootstrap the project context.
evals.expected_output: A mini-sdd/context.md file that correctly identifies the project as a Node.js/Fastify REST API, includes PostgreSQL and Vitest in the tech stack, captures key features from the README, and follows the context.template.md structure.
expectations: context.md is created at the output path
context.md follows the template structure with sections: Product, Key Features, Architecture, Tech Stack, Non-Functional Requirements
Fastify is mentioned in the tech stack
PostgreSQL is mentioned in the tech stack
Vitest is mentioned as the test runner
The Last updated date field is populated (not a placeholder like YYYY-MM-DD)
drizzle-orm or Drizzle ORM is mentioned in the document

evals.prompt: I just finished implementing user authentication with JWT tokens and a PostgreSQL users table. Update the project context to reflect these changes.
evals.expected_output: The existing mini-sdd/context.md is updated: JWT auth and PostgreSQL are added to tech stack/features/components, the Last updated date becomes 2026-03-30, and all unchanged sections are preserved verbatim.
expectations: context.md is written to the output path
The Last updated date is 2026-03-30
JWT or jsonwebtoken is mentioned in the document
PostgreSQL is mentioned in the tech stack or architecture section
MongoDB is still present in the document (original data not lost)
bcrypt or password hashing is mentioned
All four original features are preserved: Product catalog, Stock level tracking, Reorder alerts, CSV import/export

evals.prompt: I want to use mini-SDD on this project. Set up the context file.
evals.expected_output: A mini-sdd/context.md is created for the Python CLI dbsnap tool. The skill handles the absence of a README gracefully, infers project info from pyproject.toml and main.py, asks targeted questions, and produces a complete context file.
expectations: context.md is created at the output path (skill did not crash or refuse due to missing README)
dbsnap is mentioned as the project name
PostgreSQL or psycopg2 is mentioned in the tech stack
click is mentioned in the tech stack
Both snapshot and diff commands are mentioned as features or key capabilities
The document is complete (no placeholder sections are missing — unknown fields are either inferred or explicitly marked)
